AUD,"Accounting and Review Service Engagements, Interim Reviews, and Ethics and Professional Responsibilities",Ethical and Independence Requirements: Part 1,A-06-08,"Generally, independence rules apply to a covered member and their spouse and dependents.  According to SEC rules, what independence rules apply to close relatives?","

According to SEC rules, independence is impaired if the close family member:

  • has an accounting role or financial reporting oversight role with the SEC audit client (e.g., the family member is a treasurer, CFO, accounting supervisor, or controller).
  • has beneficial ownership of more than 5 percent of a client's equity securities or controls the client.
  • has a direct investment in stocks, bonds, notes, options, or other securities of the audit client.
  • has a material indirect investment in the audit client.
  • serves as a voting trustee or a trust or executor of an estate containing securities of the audit client.
"

AUD,"Accounting and Review Service Engagements, Interim Reviews, and Ethics and Professional Responsibilities",Ethical and Independence Requirements: Part 1,A-06-08,"Under SOX Title II, what services may not be provided to an audit client?","

Prohibited services include:

  • Bookkeeping
  • Financial information systems design and implementation
  • Appraisal and valuation services
  • Actuarial services
  • Management functions and human resources services
  • Internal audit outsourcing services
  • Services as a broker, dealer, investment advisor, or investment banker
  • Legal services
  • Expert services unrelated to the audit

Tax services are permissible if preapproved by the audit committee.

"

What are the steps to evaluate auditor independence under GAGAS?

,"
  1. Identification of threats to independence.
  2. Evaluation of the significance of threats identified both individually and in the aggregate.
  3. Application of safeguards necessary to eliminate the threats or reduce them to an acceptable level.

If no safeguards are available to eliminate an unacceptable threat or reduce it to an acceptable level, independence would be considered to be impaired.

"

AUD,"Accounting and Review Service Engagements, Interim Reviews, and Ethics and Professional Responsibilities",Ethical and Independence Requirements: Part 2,A-06-09,"

Under generally accepted government auditing standards, what is the critical consideration in determining whether a non-audit service is a threat to independence?

,

Consideration of management’s ability to effectively oversee the non-audit service to be performed. 

The auditor should determine:

  1. that the audited entity has designated an individual who possesses suitable skill, knowledge, or experience; and
  2. that the individual understands the services to be performed sufficiently to oversee them.

The individual is not required to possess the expertise to perform or reperform the services. The auditor should document consideration of management's ability to effectively oversee non-audit services to be performed.

"

Name two methods of accounting for the recognition of credit loss adjustments (write-offs) of outstanding accounts receivable.

Direct Write-off

FAR,Assets and Related Topics,Trade Receivables,F-03-02,

What is the difference between factoring with recourse and without recourse?

,

With Recourse
The factor may return the account to the company if it subsequently requires a credit loss adjustment. Potential liability and risk of loss remains with the company.

Without Recourse
The factor assumes the risk of loss if the account requires a credit loss adjustment (write-off).

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Filing Requirements and Filing Status,R-01-01,

At what taxable income level is an individual taxpayer who is not married filing separately required to file a federal income tax return?

,

An individual taxpayer who is not married filing separately must generally file a federal income tax return if the taxpayer’s taxable income is equal to or greater than the sum of:

  • the regular standard deduction amount; plus
  • the additional standard deduction amount for taxpayers age 65 or older.

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Filing Requirements and Filing Status,R-01-01,

What is the due date for individual taxpayers to file a calendar-year federal income tax return?

,"

The due date for calendar-year individual income tax returns is April 15 of the following year.

  • If April 15 falls on a Saturday, Sunday, or legal holiday, the due date is the next business day.
  • Automatic six-month extension (until October 15) of time to file (not extension of time to pay).
"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Filing Requirements and Filing Status,R-01-01,"

For children of divorced parents, which parent is entitled to claim the child as a dependent for tax purposes?

","

The parent who has physical custody of the child for the greater number of days during the tax year qualifies to claim the child as a dependent for tax purposes.

  • Dependency is determined by time, not the divorce decree.
  • A parent does not have to provide more than one-half of the child’s support for the child to qualify as a dependent.
  • If the parents have equal physical custody during the year, the parent with the higher AGI is entitled to claim the child as a dependent for tax purposes.
"

For a property settlement in a divorce, the transferring spouse gets no deduction for payments made (or property transferred), and the payments are not includable in the gross income of the spouse receiving the payment or property.  This is still true even if the divorce was finalized on or before December 31, 2018.

column2: • If rented fewer than 15 days: treat as personal residence.

,"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Gross Income: Part 2,R-01-03,

,

  • Disallowed business interest expense can be carried forward indefinitely.
  • Limitation does not apply if average gross receipts are $31 million or less (2025) for the prior three taxable years.

Which is a deduction for AGI: child support or alimony?

,"

Child support is not deductible by the payor or taxable to the recipient.

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Adjustments,R-01-04,

What are the limits on contributions to SEP IRA plans?

,"

SEP IRA plans are retirement plans for self-employed (SE) taxpayers and their employees. Only the employer, not the employee, can make contributions to an employee SEP IRA. 

The maximum employer contribution to an employee's SEP IRA in 2025 is the lesser of 25 percent of the employee's compensation or $70,000.

The maximum contribution to a self-employed taxpayer's own SEP IRA in 2025 is the lesser of:

  • 25 percent of net income from self-employment reduced by the SEP IRA contribution itself, which effectively equals 20 percent of net income from self-employment after the deduction for self-employment tax; or
  • $70,000.

No additional catch-up contributions for taxpayers age 50 or older are allowed for SEP IRAs.

"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Adjustments,R-01-04,

What is the maximum annual contribution a taxpayer can make to IRAs?

,"

The maximum annual contribution a taxpayer can make to IRAs for 2025 is the lesser of:

  • $7,000 ($8,000 if age 50 or older); or
  • earned income (can use spouse’s earned income if MFJ).

Annual contribution limit applies to the sum of a taxpayer’s contributions to:

  • Deductible traditional IRAs
  • Nondeductible traditional IRAs
  • Roth IRAs
"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Adjustments,R-01-04,

What are the limits on deduction of traditional IRA contributions?

,"

AGI limitations (phase-outs) apply to the deduction of traditional IRA contributions if a taxpayer (or spouse, if married) participates in an employer-sponsored retirement plan (ESRP). 2025 AGI phase-out ranges:

  • Unmarried taxpayer participates in an ESRP: AGI $79,000–$89,000
  • Married taxpayer participates in an ESRP: AGI $126,000–$146,000
  • Married taxpayer does not participate in an ESRP, but spouse does: AGI $236,000–$246,000
  • If a taxpayer (and spouse, if married) does not participate in an ESRP: No AGI limit on deduction of traditional IRA contributions
"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Adjustments,R-01-04,

What are the limits on contributions to a Roth IRA?

,"

Contributions to a Roth IRA are subject to the following limitations in 2025:

  • Maximum contribution limits for all IRAs are the lesser of:
    • $7,000 ($8,000 if age 50 or older); or
    • earned income (can use spouse’s earned income if MFJ).
  • AGI limitations (phase-outs) for Roth IRA contributions only:
    • Unmarried: AGI phase-out $150,000–$165,000
    • MFJ: AGI phase-out $236,000–$246,000
"

REG,Federal Taxation of Individuals,Tax Computations and Credits,R-01-07,"

What is the tax treatment of unearned income of a child who falls under the ""kiddie tax"" rules?

","

Net unearned income of a dependent child who falls under the ""kiddie tax"" rules is taxed at the parent's marginal rate.

Net unearned income = Child's total unearned income less:

  • the child's standard deduction of $1,350 (2025); and
  • an additional $1,350 (taxed at the child's tax rate in 2025).

The amount of unearned income that is taxed at the parent's rate is unearned income in excess of $2,700 (2025), even if the child is entitled to a larger standard deduction.

"

REG,Property Taxation,Basis and Holding Period of Assets,R-02-01,"

In general, how is the donee's basis of a gift determined? How is the holding period determined?

","

column2: • In general, the donee's basis of a gift is the same as the donor's basis. The carryover basis may be increased for gift tax paid on appreciation of the gift.
• The holding period includes the donor's holding period unless basis becomes FMV, then the holding period starts at date of gift.

column1:
REG,Property Taxation,Basis and Holding Period of Assets,R-02-01,

How is the basis and holding period of inherited property determined?

,"

The basis of inherited property is the fair market value (FMV) at the date of the decedent's death, unless the alternate valuation date is validly elected, in which case the basis is:

  • the FMV six months after the date of death; or
  • the FMV on the date of distribution/sale, if earlier than six months.

The holding period is automatically considered long-term for all inherited property, regardless of how long the deceased owned the property.

"

REG,Property Taxation,Basis and Holding Period of Assets,R-02-01,"

What is the exclusion amount on the recognition of gain on the sale of a personal residence, provided the criteria for exclusion are met?

","

  • $250,000 for single, married filing separately, and head of household taxpayers
  • $500,000 for married filing jointly taxpayers
"

REG,Property Taxation,Basis and Holding Period of Assets,R-02-01,

What is the basis of gifted property for determining gain or loss in a sale transaction?

,
  • If FMV at date of gift > donor's rollover basis: use rollover basis.
  • If FMV at date of gift < donor's rollover basis:
    • If sales price > rollover basis: use rollover basis.
    • If sales price < lower FMV at date of gift: use FMV of date of gift.
    • If sales price < rollover basis but > FMV at date of gift: use basis = sales price
      (no gain or loss recognized).

REG,Property Taxation,Basis and Holding Period of Assets,R-02-01,

What is the basis of gifted property for depreciation purposes?

Capital gains are fully taxable (but at lower tax rates).

,"

REG,Property Taxation,"Cost Recovery ",R-02-03,

What is the mid-quarter convention under MACRS?

,"

Mid-quarter convention replaces the half-year convention if greater than 40% of a taxpayer's personal property (3-year through 20-year classes) is placed in service during the last three months of a tax year.

The mid-quarter convention treats all personal property placed in service during each quarter of the tax year as being placed in service on the mid-point of the quarter.

"

REG,Property Taxation,"Cost Recovery ",R-02-03,

What is the mid-month convention under MACRS?

,"

Mid-month convention is used for calculating MACRS depreciation of real property:

The real property is treated as placed in service in the middle of the month of acquisition and disposed of during the middle of the month of disposition.

What is the Section 179 expense deduction?

,"

Up to $1,250,000 of acquisition cost of personal property used in a trade or business may be deducted in 2025.

Limitations:

  1. Reduced $1 for each $1 of qualifying property placed in service in excess of $3,130,000 (2025).
     
  2. Deduction is limited to taxable income (before the deduction).

REG,Property Taxation,"Cost Recovery ",R-02-03,

Describe the bonus depreciation method for federal income tax purposes.

,"
  • Bonus depreciation expense is 40 percent (2025) of the cost of qualified property placed in service during the year.
  • Qualified property is personal property and qualified improvements with a class life of 20 years or less.
  • Bonus depreciation expense is claimed after Section 179 expense (if elected), and before regular MACRS depreciation expense.
"

,"

Policies on key employees are not deductible by the corporation when the corporation is directly or indirectly the beneficiary.

If insured employees name the beneficiaries, premiums are deductible by the corporation as an employee benefit.

REG,Entity Taxation,C Corporation Tax Computations and Credits,R-03-03,

What is the accumulated earnings tax?

,

The accumulated earnings tax is a tax on accumulated earnings beyond the reasonable needs of the business.
Corporations can accumulate up to $250,000, or an amount reasonable to the needs of the business, without penalty. For personal service corporations, the amount is up to $150,000.
The tax is a flat 20% of the unreasonable accumulated earnings.

State the general NOL carryforward rules.

,
  • Net operating losses (NOLs) arising before 2018 can be carried forward 20 years.

How can S corporation status be terminated?
S corporation status will terminate as a result of the following:
• Holders of a majority of the stock consent to a voluntary termination.
• The corporation fails to meet any or all of the eligibility requirements.
• More than 25% of the corporation's gross receipts come from passive activities for three consecutive years and the corporation had C corporation earnings and profits at the end of each year.
